items.title: [正面] You'll wander through beautiful settings that look...
items.summary: You'll wander through beautiful settings that look like the work of famous surrealist artists. You'll try to figure out why this world feels like a jumble of dreams, like someone just scattered the pieces and forgot to put them back. The game is easy and slow, so you can enjoy the calm atmosphere without having to run around frantically or save the world

items.title: [正面] 总体给到人上人水平，画风剧情配音配合的都很好。还有我最喜欢它的前期福利，给的很慷慨啊，玩到现在我抽了...
items.summary: 总体给到人上人水平，画风剧情配音配合的都很好。还有我最喜欢它的前期福利，给的很慷慨啊，玩到现在我抽了400抽左右（0氪）虽然都是常驻但也够用了能组建1·2队开荒体验不错的队伍。玩法方面就是纯粹的肉鸽打牌，各种增益叠就对了。体验下来美中不足就是每一关的耗时特别长，还有些主线关卡想要完成三星卡的很极限，拿不到要重头再打很麻烦。

items.title: [正面] A stunning lovecraftian horror indie deckbuilder t...
items.summary:
A stunning lovecraftian horror indie deckbuilder that deserves its time in the spotlight

OVERALL IMPRESSION:
Just like every good mystery holds secrets beneath the surface, Morimens is more than what you initially perceive it to be. At first glance, it may appear similar to any other turned based gacha game on the market. However, through clever game design principles, stunning visual art and a captivating story that is well paced and you have all the pieces of an intriguing gaming experience. Morimens is an anime lovecraftian horror RPG with turn based deck building and some slight rogue-lite mechanics. It succeeds in the turned based deck building genre where many others have failed. Morimens’ understanding of what deck building is and giving the player the actual ability to craft decks allows for satisfying team building and strategic gameplay. This is one of the best games in its genre that almost no one has heard of or is talking about. It’s a shame because on top of being a stellar game, it’s extremely free to play friendly setting a new bar for what it means to be generous and value the player experience.

GAMEPLAY:
Exceptional game design is what has allowed Morimens to set itself apart from its many competitors. Turn based games especially in the deck building genre usually face challenges with repetitive gameplay, lack of true depth and team building/synergy that is unrestrained. Morimens solves many of these issues through their game design principles.

A core understanding of deck building
First, most deck building games don’t utilize the actual card play mechanic. Often you’ll see many card based RPGs ignore what it means to actually create a deck. Instead they’ll use simple mechanics like combining similar cards to produce a stronger version. For anyone who has played a true deck building game like Magic the Gathering you know that there’s much more to deck building and there’s also a reason to have multiple copies of a given card. You want multiple copies to increase your chance to pull a card. This balances out your draws to give your deck more consistency. Morimens understands this concept and with each character kit they have common cards that almost all units have like defense and strike cards. Meaning no matter how you put together a team you’ll have a certain number of copies of cards from each category. This step alone many games in the genre overlook.

Team Building and Meaningful Choice
The core of the gameplay revolves around deck building and the character and teams you put together. There are 4 different realms (factions) each with their own unique game mechanic and style of play. Each character comes with a set of cards that they will add to your overall deck. They also come with a unique ultimate ability (exalt). By selecting a team of up to 4 characters you will build your deck. Characters generally come with certain category of cards and gameplay mechanics are then built upon these categories. For example, by playing a strike type card it may provide a buff or trigger a certain mechanic. Because the core of the game is built upon these basic elements you can see how easily adding new characters give players more tools and more options to customize their playstyle and come up with interesting and satisfying builds. Want to stack shields or focus on poison or counterattack damage? It’s all possible by assembling the right team. Characters also come with what you expect in an RPG gacha in that duplicates of characters will unlock further traits. Characters and their skills can also level up like in a traditional RPG and can be equipped with items (Wheel of Destiny) to further customize and augment their abilities. Lastly, you as the commander also have a selection of abilities that can apply buffs/debuffs for your team. Together, these are all the necessary building blocks that enable creative, strategic and meaningful choices for the player.

STORY, ART, & CHARACTER DESIGN
Morimens pulls you into its lovecraftian world through it’s visual design and narrative. Exceptional art has the power to draw you in. Art design can take a backseat to many other aspects of a game, but it’s usually the starting point that captivates your attention in order to decipher if further investigation is warranted. The character and art design in this game are imaginative, unique and well executed. One of the most challenging aspects of creating a new IP is developing something that is distinct and memorable. The visual design here is some of the best in its genre. It’s a world that is intriguing and warrants further investigation.

The story follows the main protagonist (the player), a keeper of the Mythag University that investigates phenomenon in a falling world with cosmic horror vibes. Much of the story unfolds like a mystery as you discover more about the world and uncover strange occurrences. Themes from the lovecraftian genre are also present here ranging from humanities conflict with cosmic forces, madness, fate and despair. Despite the English version being translated from chatGPT, the overall story is fascinating, well paced and each chapter is the right duration to hold the audiences attention. These elements altogether are usually missing in many narratives and can impact the audiences ability to connect with a story. But when these critical components are all present, it makes for a tale that can pull you into its world making you to want to delve further into its vast array of questions and mystery.

Character design is also equally intriguing and matches well with the overall art design and narrative. The world and characters have their own unique aesthetic with a mix of gothic, medieval, anime and lovecraftian themes. There’s also a good mix of both plot and character moments. Many of the characters you meet along the journey have intriguing, at times sad and dark backstories that make the world feel authentic and layered with depth. These are some of my favorite character designs I’ve come across in a game in a long time. Throughout the game, the developers have demonstrated their artistic vision, displaying true craftsmanship and respect for their work and world building.

GACHA ELEMENTS
This is by far one of the most generous and friendly gacha games on the market currently. The pull rates and pity threshold (30 pulls) are better than most gachas on the market. On top of that you can obtain character and item selectors that allow you to straight up select a unit that you want to add to your team. As a new player, you’ll likely come across several of these allowing you to put together a team of your choice. On top of that, the currency you can earn from playing the game is also quite generous allowing you to gain access to a lot of pulls. This is a step in the right direction for the genre as a whole.

CHALLENGES
Similar to most games in the genre, overall progression is slow and it is still affected by the gacha model. The biggest challenges for most of these type of games in this genre is that it can eventually feel like work to keep up with all the daily/weekly rewards in order to have currency for pulls. Most of the games eventually exhaust players due to how they are setup. In the early stages its exciting to pull for characters but eventually once you get a good lineup new units aren't as appealing. This is more a factor of the monetization model that in many ways goes against  a good player experience. 

CLOSING THOUGHTS
Morimens is an incredible decking building game set in a cosmic horror world with plenty of strategic and meaningful choices when it comes to team building. It sets itself apart in its genre by truly understanding what it takes to create a deck building game with enough depth and customization to avoid the many challenges the genre faces. Combined with exceptional and distinct art design and an intriguing and well paced narrative and you have all the makings of an incredible experience.
